headline: RM1,500 Minimum Wage — On Paper
context: Malaysia raised the minimum wage to RM1,500 in May 2022, but enforcement covers less than half the workforce.

cards.big: Minimum wage is RM1,500. Actual median pay for 1.2M workers: RM1,100.
cards.sub: The gap between law and payslip is not a rounding error.

cards.big: Labour Department has 487 inspectors for 900,000 employers.
cards.sub: One inspector per 1,848 workplaces. Average inspection cycle: seven years.

cards.big: 73% of violations found in SMEs with fewer than five employees.
cards.sub: Penalties max at RM10,000 — less than a year of underpayment savings.

cards.big: Domestic workers and gig workers are excluded entirely.
cards.sub: Employment Act 1955 definitions have not kept pace with the labour market.

cards.big: A minimum wage nobody enforces is a suggestion, not a law.
cards.sub: The question is whether Malaysia has a labour inspectorate or a labour theatre.

cards.big: Rights without enforcement are promises. Malaysia legislates ambition but funds compliance at a fraction of need.
